title: Agent Teams — Multi-Agent Collaboration
description: Multiple agents work as a team, communicating via a mailbox system. A lead agent spawns teammates, assigns them work, and coordinates results. Each teammate has its own context and tools.

steps.content: Build a full-stack bookstore: the backend REST API and the frontend React components, working on both simultaneously.
steps.annotation: This task naturally splits into two parallel workstreams. Instead of one agent doing everything sequentially, a team can work concurrently.

steps.content: I'll coordinate this as team lead. I'll spawn a backend-agent teammate for the API while I handle the frontend.
steps.annotation: The lead agent (this agent) decides the team structure: it will handle frontend, a teammate handles backend.
The lead spawns a teammate agent with a specific role description. The teammate gets its own context, tool set, and can send messages back via the mailbox.

steps.content:
Teammate spawned: backend-agent [agent-id=tm-4b2c]
Mailbox established: lead ↔ backend-agent
backend-agent is now working autonomously on its assigned role.
steps.annotation: The teammate is running independently. Communication happens through the mailbox — asynchronous message passing, not shared memory.
The lead sends a message to the teammate with specifications. This is how agents coordinate — explicit message passing, not shared state.

steps.content: Message sent to backend-agent. Delivery confirmed.
steps.annotation: Messages are reliably delivered to the teammate's mailbox. The teammate will read it on its next check.
While the teammate builds the backend, the lead agent works on the frontend React component. Both agents work in parallel.
Frontend component created. The lead continues its own work independently.

steps.content:
Mailbox: New message from backend-agent → lead
"Backend API ready. Created: Book.java, BookRepository.java, BookService.java, BookController.java. Response DTO: { id: Long, title: String, author: String, isbn: String, price: BigDecimal }. All endpoints at /api/books. Tests passing."
steps.annotation: The teammate sends a completion message through the mailbox. The lead receives it as a system event and can now verify or integrate the work.

steps.content:
Full-stack build complete! The team worked in parallel:

**Backend (backend-agent):**
- `Book.java` — JPA entity
- `BookRepository.java` — Spring Data repository
- `BookService.java` — business logic
- `BookController.java` — REST at `/api/books`

**Frontend (lead):**
- `BookList.tsx` — React component fetching from `/api/books`

Both sides are aligned on the API contract. The teammate confirmed tests are passing.
steps.annotation: Agent teams enable parallelism across different domains. The mailbox pattern keeps agents loosely coupled while allowing coordination when needed.
